messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
Which movie got Vivien Liegh the Oscar for best actress in 1952

content.message:
I need to find which movie won Vivien Liegh the Oscar for best actress in 1952.

First, I need to clarify: I believe there might be a typo. "Vivien Liegh" is likely meant to be "Vivien Leigh" - a well-known actress. I'll proceed with "Vivien Leigh" as that is the correct name.

Let me first search for Vivien Leigh in Wikidata to confirm her entity.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Vivien Leigh (wd:Q129429), also known as "Lady Olivier", "Leigh", "Vivien Leigh, Lady Olivier":
  - British actress
  - has occupation actor
  - has occupation film actor
  - has occupation stage actor
  - is instance of human
2. Book:Vivien Leigh (wd:Q20980695):
  - is instance of Wikipedia:Books
3. Category:Vivien Leigh (wd:Q55248997):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
4. Vivien Leigh (1913–1967) (wd:Q119247865):
  - is instance of painting
  - painting by Thomas Cantrell Dugdale (1880–1952), Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum
5. Vivian, Herbert Leigh (wd:Q94970970):
  - entry in Alumni Oxonienses: the Members of the University of Oxford, 1715-1886
  - is instance of biographical article
6. Leigh, Vivien (1913-1967), actress (wd:Q103867257):
  - encyclopedia article
  - is instance of encyclopedia article
7. Goddess Vivian Leigh (wd:Q117829916), also known as "Vivian The Domina":
  - American pornographic actor
  - has occupation pornographic actor
  - is instance of human
8. Vivian Lei (wd:Q102333020):
  - has occupation dermatologist
  - is instance of human
  - researcher
9. Vivian Lei (wd:Q102880953), also known as "Wen-chieh Lei":
  - has occupation economist
  - is instance of human
  - Ph.D. Purdue University 2000
10. Vivien Leigh: Scarlett and Beyond (wd:Q123288603):
  - 1990 film
  - is instance of film

content.message:
I've confirmed that Vivien Leigh is a well-known actress in Wikidata (wd:Q129429). Now I need to find her Oscar win for best actress in 1952.

First, let me search for the property that relates to Oscar awards, specifically best actress.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "awardee"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
2. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "awards"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
3. grants (wd:P5460 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "awards"), also known as "awards", "bestows", "confers", "gives out":
  - confers degree, honor, award, prize, title, certificate or medal denoting achievement to a person or organization
4. trophy awarded (wd:P4622 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "cup awarded"), also known as "cup awarded", "diploma awarded", "medal awarded":
  - trophy awarded at the end of a selection process or of a ... or finishers under the form of a cup, a medal, a diploma
5. nominee (wd:P2453 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award nominee"), also known as "award nominee":
  - qualifier used with “nominated for” to specify which person or organization was nominated
6. Academy Awards Database nominee ID (wd:P6150 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Oscar nominee ID"), also known as "Oscar nominee ID":
  - identifier for nominees in the official Academy Awards database
7. soundtrack release (wd:P406 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "OST"), also known as "has soundtrack album", "OST", "soundtrack album", "soundtrack extended play", "soundtrack single":
  - music release that incorporates music directly recorded from the soundtrack of an audiovisual work
8. featured track(s) (wd:P10664 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "OST"), also known as "audio track", "audio tracks", "featured track", "music track", "music tracks", etc.:
  - soundtrack of this audiovisual work
9. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject
10. number of wins (wd:P1355 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "won"), also known as "games won", "matches won", "match victories", "sporting wins", "victories", etc.:
  - number of sporting matches, games or events won

4. filming location (wd:P915 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "film place"), also known as "filmed at", "filmed in", "filmed on location in", "filming place", "film location", etc.:

8. nominee (wd:P2453 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award nominee"), also known as "award nominee":
  - qualifier used with “nominated for” to specify which person or organization was nominated
9. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award won"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
10. European Film Awards ID (wd:P8800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):

content.message: I need to find the specific Oscar for Best Actress. Let me search for the property that links to film awards specifically for Best Actress.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p, matched via "awards"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
2. Academy Awards Database nominee ID (wd:P6150 as wdt/p, matched via "Oscar nominee ID"), also known as "Oscar nominee ID":
  - identifier for nominees in the official Academy Awards database
3. nominated for (wd:P1411 as wdt/p, matched via "selected for award"), also known as "finalist for", "finalist for award", "have nomination to", "nominated for award", "nomination received", etc.:
  - award nomination received by a person, organisation or creative work (inspired from "award received" (Property:P166))
4. child (wd:P40 as wdt/p, matched via "son"), also known as "children", "daughter", "daughters", "descendants", "father of", etc.:
  - subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
5. unmarried partner (wd:P451 as wdt/p, matched via "SO"), also known as "boyfriend", "cohabitant", "domestic partner", "enbyfriend", "girlfriend", etc.:
  - someone with whom the person is in a relationship without being married. Use "spouse" (P26) for married couples
6. birth name (wd:P1477 as wdt/p, matched via "né"), also known as "birthname", "bn", "bname", "born as", "full birth name", etc.:
  - full name of a person at birth, if different from their current, generally used name
7. Oxford Dictionary of National Biography ID (wd:P1415 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "OBIN"), also known as "OBIN", "ODNB ID", "Oxford Biography Index Number":
  - identifier used by the Oxford Dictionary of National Biography
8. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
9. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
